item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes that is very interesting due to the fact that when looking at it's face it goes in a traumatized expression and seeks out to kill. This is confusing due to the fact that no one knows why his face is so angering to shy guy, and why it has the intent to kill. Also how it came to the world, and it's strange shape.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting take on this, but what if 096 is cursed? it would be interesting if this entity isn't killing purely out of rage, but out of mercy upon those who would suffer the curse of seeing its face. what if it kills you because of whatever would happen if it didn't?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just wanted to tell you about collapsible boxes.
[[collapsible show="ALIEN SPOILER" hide="Spoiled"]]
[[/collapsible]]
Like this:

Harry Potter spoilers
Spoiled

Dumbledore is killed by Snape

item.title: Re: Does 096 have an origin story?
item.content:encoded:
It's up to your interpretation.

You could always write one if you have a particularly good idea.

Edit: this was a bit of a necro my bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cognitohazards are objects that are dangerous to sense or perceive, whether through sight, sound, smell, taste, or feeling. Perceiving 096's face is hazardous, therefore, it is a cognitohazard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is this tagged *cognitohazard?*

item.title: Re: Does 096 have an origin story?
item.content:encoded: I don't think it's an official origin story but it's a good theory

item.title: Re: Does 096 have an origin story?
item.content:encoded: The YouTube channel SCP Exploration has a pretty good origin story for him and other SCPs. Maybe give it a look

item.title: Does 096 have an origin story?
item.content:encoded:
Is there an origin story of SCP-096? I've always wondered.

To me, it seems to be a victim of some greek mythic kind of curse.
Like how Medusa turns people into stone.. So this creature might have been some beautifull, narcistic demi-god and was cursed to [DATA EXPUNGED] anyone who viewed his ugly face.

item.title: How to fix SCRAMBLE goggles
item.content:encoded: The footage doesn't have to be live. A one second delay every time 096's face appear should be enough time for the censorship to block out anything dangerous, but even a half second delay would work as long as it doesn't display his face at all.

item.title: SCP-096-01
item.content:encoded: How would it be knowledgeable of what SCP-096 would do to someone if they saw it if it has never happened? So it has already happened at least twice otherwise knowing that distance is not a factor would be unknown. I love the article, I just noticed these "errors" which aren't that big of a deal.

item.title: regeneration
item.content:encoded:
does SCP-096 have some sort of quick regeneration so when its skin and flesh it comes back within a day, in most cross tests they mention it has regenerative abilities, and i'm just wondering if that was intended from the start, or not intended at all.

P.s. this is my favorite SCP because of how terrifying it is

item.title: Re: Creator
item.content:encoded: Dr Dan

item.title: Re: Termination
item.content:encoded: It will attempt to kill itself, but it will be unsuccessful.

item.title: Re: mask
item.content:encoded: It would take it off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-096 and SCP-049 receive the #1 bromance award.

item.title: What SCP needed an anti-tank gun?
item.content:encoded: Like for real.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As time goes by, I find myself more and more disinterested in invulnerable things that kill you (682 notwithstanding, because that's what its whole shtick is about). The reasons for that one to kill people may be a bit more original than usual, but that is not enough for me to find it interesting any more.

item.title: April fools got me on the floor crying
item.content:encoded:
he turned himself into a cactus
funniest shit I've ever seen

item.title: 096 got a movie!
item.content:encoded:
I wish there were more digital contents like movies and tv shows on SCP universe

Congrats to the author of this article :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We don't have a hall of fame. If you're referring to the Heritage List, that was discontinued awhile ago.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
With the new 096 short film by MrKlay and some others, i'm still wondering to this day

why is this not in the hall of fame?

item.title: Re: How to stop 096 in 5 easy ways!
item.content:encoded: But he is up for termination. That's what the text says.

item.title: How to stop 096 in 5 easy ways!
item.content:encoded:
1: put his box on the next space x rocket and send it into the sun!
2: give his box to 001 angel guardian! (Though 001 can just give him the command to go away or somethin)
3: mirror!
4: put him in one of those dimension scps! (Like that cave, the doorway, etc)
5: pit him in one of those diseased areas like the flesh mutant one

The only problem would be that he isn't up for termination

item.title: Creator
item.content:encoded: Do you guys know the user who created the 096 page?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *Ducktales Moon Theme starts playing

item.title: Termination
item.content:encoded: what if we put a mirror in from of it.

item.title: Re: Waiiiit...
item.content:encoded: maybe one of those cars that can go 400 mph and have a jet engine.

item.title: Re: SCP-096 and Amnestic
item.content:encoded: It will kill them. Remember, SCP-096 can hunt you even when you don't realize you've seen its face (the whole 4 pixels story).

item.title: Re: Question
item.content:encoded: If you click History at the bottom of the page and go the last page, the person with the first revision almost always wrote the article. For 096 it was a user named Dr Dan.

item.title: Re: Question
item.content:encoded:
Who originally wrote this article?

Dr Dan created the page.

I can never find who wrote them when I'm on the page, unless it's meant to be that way?

You need to open up the History option at the bottom of the page, or use the chatbot to look up the authors, or the scpper database. Author names are not listed in SCP articles.

item.title: Question
item.content:encoded: Who originally wrote this article? It's weird but I can never find who wrote them when I'm on the page, unless it's meant to be that way?

item.title: SCP-096 and Amnestic
item.content:encoded:
So if someone saw SCP-096 face then given amnestic, I assume SCP-096 will still try to kill that person? Or will it ignore that person?
This Youtube question kind of haunts me.

item.title: Re: So what would happen if...
item.content:encoded:
Bruh, I watched SCP containtment breach game and it happened.
I laughed for several minutes because the situation was just so bad.

item.title: SCP 4498
item.content:encoded: Just came from SCP 4498. Now I can't help but call this one Jack Bright in my head every time I read this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Blew. My. Mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well yeah, but how would they "seal the door"? Even a titanium door would be nothing for 96 to get through.

item.title: Re: Question
item.content:encoded: I think that what would probably happen is that 96 would just be very,very confused. It may eventually just attack the corpse anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. this hurts to read
2. it would kill initial bystanders before they could upload a picture anywhere, hence the incident report.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How would the Foundation get it there?
I appreciate the idea, but there are some problems.

  1. Although the Foundation has a massive budget (they hardly even need one to get things done) it would be a waste of time and resources to transport it there.
  2. Also, and this is kind of ludicrous, the Foundation knows that there could be life out there- and they could be endangering it.
  3. It would be easier just (if initial termination attempts failed) if they kept it where it was and permanently sealed the door, then amnesticized all previously assigned personnel to prevent them from knowing about it.

It is, however, a very creative and interesting idea.

item.title: Re: Question
item.content:encoded: It says that in all cases, there are no traces left from the viewer, so if someone were to die before 096 gets to them, 096 would probably just get rid of the viewer's corpse

item.title: Question
item.content:encoded: What if someone who viewed SCP-96 dies before he can get to them first? Does he enter his docile state? Or does he go after the person who killed the viewer? If they were killed by non living things such as a rock, what would happen?

item.title: mask
item.content:encoded: Wouldnt just be much simpler to make it wear a mask?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome story building.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: nah in one of the logs four pixels made it get angry so even a blurred face would kill the person

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is just an Enderman with extra steps
__

CHANGE MY MIND

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well it DID take down a plane midair

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If we put -096 on some really far away planet we could just leave him there. I'm thinking light years away. Even if he could escape the planet, it would still take still take him an ungodly amount of time for him to get back to earth. The person who saw his face might be dead by the time -096 gets back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I now have an image in my head ALIEN SPOILER of the scene at the end of the movie Alien when the Xenomorph was flushed into space, but instead it's with SCP-096.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
actchually scp-96 is just a white enderman, so all yu need to do is wear a pumpkin head and youre good :]

But in all serious I have no idea if that'd actually work. I think it still wouldn't work out somehow because there would be that .000001 fraction of a second where you do see it's face.

item.title: Re: Termination
item.content:encoded: There really doesn't need to be a termination log/story. All we really need to know is that it will be terminated at some distant time in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had an idea for how to make the helmets that block shy guys face work. All you have to do is delay the footage relayed to the goggles by quarter of a second, then the computer would have time to censor Shy guy's face. It would effect the MTF's reaction speed, but the disadvantages would be tiny compared to safe viewing of 096.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. ███: That's when you ordered the use of an [Papers are heard moving] AT-4 HEDT launcher?

Why is this funny?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What would happen if someone targeted by 096 was killed by someone else? Would SCP-096 return to a docile state?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: well you can easily contain just by keeping it locked up and no one ever look at it again. That's why its euclid

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
TIL 096 is a euclid…
Makes no sense considering one boi looking at it could kill the entire world.
Imagine…..
*guy looks at image* ooh creepy
SCP 096: hello mf *rips dude up*
Bystander: umm ok *takes picture and posts on instagram*
096:excuse me what the fuck *kills picture boi*
Hundreds of insta bois: ooh creepy
096 :excuse me what the fuck x100 *kills all*
News boi: look it spooky monster owo
All news watchers: owo spooky
096: fuck I told you NOT to FUCKING LOOK* kill*
All of a sudden everybody FUCKING DIES BECAUSE FUCKING 096 IS KETER

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WATCH OUT WATCH OUT WHOA

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could it swim in space though…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
096 is a precious baby boy and deserves all the love.

Just give him a blindfold and I'm sure he's good at hugs.

item.title: Re: catapult 096 into orbit
item.content:encoded:
"do you hear that?"

"No… probably nothing"
